title: OpenAI introduces GPT-Live: ChatGPT's voice mode enables more human-like real-time conversations
summary: The new voice feature listens and responds without pauses, improving the fluidity of interactions with the AI.

title: SpaceXAI launches Grok 4.5 considered an Opus-class model with competitive pricing
summary: The new AI model offers performance comparable to GPT 5.5 and Fable 5 but at a significantly lower cost.

title: Meta tightens privacy measures on its smart glasses after complaints about covert recording
summary: The company updates its policies to prevent users from covering the recording indicator LED, while testing glasses that capture all day.

title: Meta launches Muse Image, an AI model to generate and edit images in Instagram and WhatsApp
summary: The tool uses Instagram photos to create advanced visual effects, sparking debate about data privacy.

title: Mistral presents Robostral Navigate, a robotic navigation model that uses a single camera
summary: The 8 billion parameter model enables robots to orient themselves with a single camera, marking a breakthrough in autonomous robotics.

title: Cooler Master V4 and V8 3DHP: a masterpiece of cooling engineering
summary: The new heat sinks with 3DHP technology offer exceptional performance according to early reviews.

title: AMD announces its new generation of leading x86 CPUs alongside X870 motherboards
summary: The company announces its Advancing AI event for July, while listings of the ASUS ROG Crosshair X870E Edition appear.

title: Details of Intel Nova Lake CPUs with Xe3 and Xe3P graphics leaked
summary: The next-generation processors promise a big leap in graphics performance according to revealed specifications.

title: Valve releases drivers to run Windows on Steam Machine, but offers no official support
summary: The drivers allow installing Windows on Steam hardware, although Valve warns it will not provide technical support.

title: US deploys a high-power mobile electromagnetic anti-satellite weapon
summary: The US Space Force activates the 'Meadowlands' system to disable enemy satellites with electromagnetic rays.

title: DLSS Swapper modding tool could contain malware when downloading third-party DLL files
summary: The app's creator warns against using user-submitted DLLs that could infect the system.

title: Massive data leak exposes millions of driver's license numbers
summary: A new security breach compromises sensitive personal information of drivers in the United States.

title: 15-year-old GhostLock vulnerability allows privilege escalation in most Linux distributions
summary: The kernel flaw enables container escape and root access, affecting older Linux systems.

title: Budget smartphone market contracts 22% due to memory shortage
summary: Memory cost accounts for up to 64% of the total price of low-end devices, causing a sales drop.

title: SpaceX and City Labs launch the first commercial nuclear satellite on Transporter-17 mission
summary: The milestone opens the door to new space applications with onboard nuclear power sources.

title: Samsung achieves 28.4 GB/s with PCIe 6.0 SSD transferring 100 GB in 3.5 seconds
summary: The new storage standard doubles the bandwidth of the previous generation, approaching 30 GB/s.

title: Sony CEO fuels rumors about a possible PlayStation Vita 2
summary: Statements from the executive suggest that the company is considering reviving its handheld console.

title: Chrome 150 adds new CSS properties, fixes 382 bugs, and continues removing Manifest V2
summary: The new version of the browser includes improvements to the rendering engine and critical security patches.

title: Future NVIDIA RTX 50 Super leaked with TGP between 10 and 17% higher
summary: Seasonic power supply calculators list unannounced models of the RTX 50 Super series.

title: Former DeepMind executive warns that the AI arms race could end in disaster
summary: The expert fears that uncontrolled competition among AI companies could lead to catastrophic consequences for humanity.
